title: Relate decimals and fractions in words
description: Practice relating equivalent decimals and fractions written in word form.
topic_path: Decimals and fractions in word form
Relate decimals and fractions in words

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following numbers are equivalent to $3$ hundredths?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
### Fraction form

Let's use a picture to see what $\blueD{ 3 \text{ hundredths}}$ looks like. 

![A square with 3 of 100 equal parts shaded](web+graphie:${â˜£ LOCALPATH}/images/dfef20b6bc76cafb5e02597c066b75980bd410d3) 

$\blueD{ 3}$ of the $\blueD{100}$ pieces are shaded.  We can write this as the fraction, $\blueD{\dfrac{3}{100}}$.

###Decimal form

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{ 3 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | $0$ | $\blueD{3}$

$\blueD{3 \text{ hundredths}} = \blueD{0.03}$

The following numbers are equivalent to $\blueD{3 \text{ hundredths}}$:

- $\blueD{\dfrac{3}{100}}$  
  
- $\blueD{0.03}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following numbers are equivalent to $54$ hundredths?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
### Fraction form

Let's use a picture to see what $\blueD{ 54 \text{ hundredths}}$ looks like. 

![A square with 54 of 100 equal parts shaded](web+graphie:${â˜£ LOCALPATH}/images/e6073f28828aff740e850aa98616c4b9c4875261) 

$\blueD{ 54}$ of the $\blueD{100}$ pieces are shaded.  We can write this as the fraction, $\blueD{\dfrac{54}{100}}$.

###Decimal form

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{ 54 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | $\blueD5$ | $\blueD{4}$

$\blueD{54 \text{ hundredths}} = \blueD{0.54}$

The following numbers are equivalent to $\blueD{54 \text{ hundredths}}$:

- $\blueD{\dfrac{54}{100}}$  
  
- $\blueD{0.54}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the written form of each number.**

[[â˜ƒ categorizer 1]]
hints:
### $\blueD{0.4}$

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{0.4}$ in written form.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\blueD0$|$.$ | $\blueD{4}$|

$\blueD{0.4}=\blueD{4\text{ tenths}}$

### $\greenD{\dfrac{4}{10}}$

Let's use a picture to see what $\greenD{\dfrac{4}{10}}$ looks like. 


[[â˜ƒ image 1]]

$\greenD{ 4}$ of the $\greenD{10}$ pieces are shaded.  We can write this as $\greenD{ 4\text{ tenths}}$.

### $\maroonD{0.04}$

We can use a place value chart to write $\maroonD{0.04}$ in written form.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\maroonD0$|$.$ | $\maroonD0$ | $\maroonD{4}$

$\maroonD{0.04}=\maroonD{4\text{ hundredths}}$

The figure below shows the written form of each number.

[[â˜ƒ categorizer 4]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the written form of each number.**

[[â˜ƒ categorizer 1]]
hints:
### $\blueD{1}$

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{1}$ in written form.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\blueD1$| | |

$\blueD{1}=\blueD{1\text{ one}}$

### $\greenD{\dfrac{1}{10}}$

Let's use a picture to see what $\greenD{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ looks like. 



[[â˜ƒ image 1]]

$\greenD{ 1}$ of the $\greenD{10}$ pieces are shaded.  We can write this as $\greenD{ 1\text{ tenth}}$.

### $\maroonD{0.01}$

We can use a place value chart to write $\maroonD{0.01}$ in written form.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\maroonD0$|$.$ | $\maroonD0$ | $\maroonD{1}$

$\maroonD{0.01}=\maroonD{1\text{ hundredth}}$

The figure below shows the written form of each number.

[[â˜ƒ categorizer 5]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the written form of each number.**

[[â˜ƒ categorizer 1]]
hints:
### $\maroonD{0.32}$

We can use a place value chart to write $\maroonD{0.32}$ in written form.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\maroonD0$|$.$ | $\maroonD3$ | $\maroonD2$

$\maroonD{0.32}=\maroonD{32\text{ hundredths}}$

### $\blueD{\dfrac{32}{100}}$

Let's use a picture to see what $\blueD{\dfrac{32}{100}}$ looks like. 


[[â˜ƒ image 1]]

$\blueD{ 32}$ of the $\blueD{100}$ pieces are shaded.  We can write this as $\blueD{ 32\text{ hundredths}}$.

### $\greenD{32}$

We can use a place value chart to write $\greenD{32}$ in written form.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths 
:-: |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\greenD3$ | $\greenD2$ | |

$\greenD{32}=\greenD{3\text{ tens and } 2\text{ ones}}$

The written form of each number is selected below.
